incorrects.description: Student placed a wrong product on first attempt

steps.text: Not quite. Skip-count by 10s. Start at 10 and count six times.

incorrects.description: Student placed a wrong product on second attempt

params.description: Equation Builder highlights the factors 6 and 10.

steps.text: 6 groups of 10. Count aloud: 10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60. What number did you land on?

incorrects.description: Student placed a wrong product three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System animates skip-counting by 10s six times: 10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60. Product slot fills with 60.

steps.text: Let me show you. 6 groups of 10. I skip-count by 10s six times: 10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60. After six counts, I land on 60. So 6 times 10 equals 60. That's the product, and it ends in zero which is the 10s pattern.
